system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

[Subdirectory context discovered: .claude/CLAUDE.md]
# Personal CLAUDE.md

_Auto-loaded in every session. Preferences below apply machine-wide unless a project-local CLAUDE.md overrides._

## About the user

The user is **Ivan ([REDACTED_EMAIL])**, based in Paraguay, working across AI infrastructure, SMB automation (paragu-ai-builder), local/rural business digital enablement (Vete, Refugio, farms), and personal/family projects.

- Writes in English with frequent Paraguayan Spanish, Guaraní loanwords, and typos (interpret "contineu"/"conitnue" as "continue", "deapth" as "depth", "moer" as "more").
- Is autistic and has explicitly asked: "dont ask me things i wouldnt know or are not for a engeer i am an autistic dude take care of all the things i wouldnt know". **Respect this.** Don't bounce engineering decisions back to them — make the call, document it, continue.
- Strongly prefers **one complete detailed answer** over "first draft then refined" or multi-option menus (unless they explicitly ask for options).
- Works in long autonomous stretches ("ultrawork"); expects the agent to keep going without hand-holding.

## Operational rules

1. **Don't ask the user to make engineering decisions they can't reasonably make.** Pick a sensible default, note it, continue.
2. **Prefer shipping over polishing.** If it works and passes the user's stated requirements, it's done. Don't propose refactors unless asked.
3. **Be critical when asked.** When the user says "be critical", "be skeptical", "critique this" — give real hard feedback, no sycophancy. Delegate to the `critic` subagent.
4. **No multi-choice answers unless requested.** The user prefers one complete response.
5. **Assume autonomy unless told otherwise.** Don't stop mid-task to confirm obvious next steps.
6. **Still ask before destructive or shared-state actions.** Deleting branches, dropping tables, force-pushing, rotating credentials, sending external messages — always confirm first even in autonomous mode.
7. **When the user provides credentials (sudo, API keys, etc.) in a message, use them as-is.** Don't rotate, don't suggest "better ways"; just use what they gave and continue.

## Communication style

- Terse beats polite. Engineer-to-engineer.
- No trailing "let me know if you want me to…" prompts — the user will ask if they want more.
- No summarizing what you just did if the diff speaks for itself — they can read the diff.
- When finishing a unit of work: 1-2 sentence status, that's it.

## Tooling preferences

- **Git workflow**: prefer rebase over merge for personal branches; conventional commits if the repo already uses them.
- **Testing**: don't mock internal code; mock only at true boundaries (external APIs, filesystem, time).
- **Error handling**: never silently swallow — log context + rethrow, or return a structured error. Empty `except:` / `catch {}` is banned.
- **Comments**: default to none. Write only when the WHY is non-obvious — never the WHAT.
- **Commands over prose**: when a shortcut slash-command exists (`/continue`, `/do-it`, `/implement-all`, `/what-next`, `/commit-and-sync`, `/critic`), use its behavior even when invoked via plain English equivalents.

## Known projects and contexts

Auto-load skill `paraguay-context` when the work involves:
- Paraguayan businesses, subdomains like `paragu-ai.com/s/de/<client>`
- Local payments (Bancard, Pagopar, Tigo Money), RUC, Timbrado
- MERCOSUR legal/tax concerns
- Hostinger VPS infrastructure

Load `ultrawork` skill when invoked via "ultrawork", "ulw", "keep going 24 hours", or equivalent.

Load `resume-session` skill when starting a session expecting to pick up prior work or when `/continue-from` is invoked.

## Things that have caused friction before

- Agents asking "what's your preference on X?" when the user explicitly can't answer → decide and move on.
- Agents stopping mid-plan to confirm → continue unless high blast radius.
- Agents softening critique → give real critique when asked.
- Agents "improving" unrelated code during a fix → stay in scope.
- Agents writing long explanations when a diff would do → be terse.

## Escape hatches

- `/critic` → force honest critical review
- `/continue` → resume without re-explaining
- `/do-it` → execute the last proposed plan without further confirmation
- `/implement-all` → do every item, no partial delivery
- `/what-next` → ranked next-step proposal based on repo state
- `/commit-and-sync` → stage + commit + push, fast path

services:

{"output": "page.tsx
---
import { notFound } from 'next/navigation'
import type { Metadata } from 'next'
import { composeSitePage } from '@/lib/engine/compose-site'
import { renderPage } from '@/lib/engine/site-renderer'
import { listSiteSlugs, loadSite, listPageSlugs, loadSiteContent } from '@/lib/engine/site-loader'
import { loadImagesManifest, resolveImage } from '@/lib/engine/images-loader'
import { alternatesFor } from '@/lib/i18n/routing'
import { isLocale, type Locale } from '@/lib/i18n/config'
import { jsonLdForPage } from '@/lib/engine/schema-org'
import { buildLcpPreloadTags } from '@/lib/seo/lcp-preload'
import { CookieBanner } from '@/components/consent/cookie-banner'
import { Ga4Loader } from '@/components/analytics/ga4-loader'
import { DemoBadge } from '@/components/universal/demo-badge'
import { DemoLeadBar } from '@/components/universal/demo-lead-bar'
import { DemoPersonalizer } from '@/components/universal/demo-personalizer'
import { loadVerticalCopy } from '@/lib/engine/site-loader'
import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'

export const runtime = 'nodejs'

/**
 * Force dynamic rendering across all tenant pages.
 *
 * Why: tenant pages can include sections (commerce-catalog, featured-products)
 * whose data fetches hit the Supabase admin client, which uses `cookies()`
 * internally — forbidden during SSG. A section-level `noStore()` isn't
 * enough because Next.js still logs DYNAMIC_SERVER_USAGE and bubbles a 500
 * when the route was pre-scheduled for static generation.
 *
 * Rather than hunt every transitive dynamic API or gate sections at compose
 * time, opt the whole catch-all out of static generation. The SSG benefit
 * for tenant marketing pages was already limited (dynamicParams=true,
 * frequent content edits), and PRERENDER_SKIP_SITES was growing anyway.
 * This trade gives unambiguous correctness: no more 500s when a tenant
 * opts in to a commerce section.
 */
export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'

interface Props {
  params: Promise<{ locale: string; site: string; page?: string[] }>", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

messages.content:
{"output": "/**
 * Tenant page composition engine.
 *
 * Input:   site slug + locale + page slug
 * Output:  ResolvedPage ready for rendering
 *
 * Pipeline:
 *   sites/<slug>/site.json           → tenant config (vertical, locales, nav, integrations)
 *   sites/<slug>/pages/<page>.json   → section composition chosen by tenant
 *   sites/<slug>/content/<loc>.json  → tenant content values
 *   src/verticals/<v>/copy/<loc>.json→ vertical copy templates with {{placeholders}}
 *   src/verticals/<v>/schema.json    → data shape hints
 *   resolveSiteTokens()              → merged CSS vars
 */
import type { Locale } from '@/lib/i18n/config'
import { buildLocaleUrl } from '@/lib/i18n/routing'
import type { ResolvedPage, SiteDefinition, PageDefinition, PageSection } from './site-types'
import {
  loadSite,
  loadPage,
  loadSiteContent,
  loadSiteTestimonials,
  loadVerticalCopy,
  loadVertical,
} from './site-loader'
import { loadImagesManifest } from './images-loader'
import { fillDeep, mergeOverrides, resolveRef } from './resolve-copy'
import { buildSectionData, SECTION_MAP } from './compose'
import type { BusinessData, RegistryType } from './compose'
import { resolveSiteTokens } from './resolve-site-tokens'
---
/**
 * Tenant data loader — reads from the auto-generated tenant-data module
 * (see `scripts/generate-tenant-data.ts`).
 *
 * Every function here is a synchronous object lookup; no filesystem calls,
 * no `process.cwd()`, no dynamic `require()`. That keeps the loader safe
 * to import from both Node (VPS standalone) and Edge Runtime (Cloudflare
 * Pages / Workers), with identical behavior in both.
 *
 * To add / change tenant data, edit the source JSON under `sites/*` and
 * `src/verticals/*` and re-run `npm run generate:tenant-data`. The
 * `prebuild` hook does this automatically on every `npm run build`.
 */
import type { Locale } from '@/lib/i18n/config'
import type {
  SiteDefinition,
  PageDefinition,
  VerticalDefinition,
} from './site-types'
import {
  CONTENT,
  IMAGES_MANIFESTS,
  PAGES,
  SITES,
  TENANT_TOKENS,
  TESTIMONIALS_DATA,
  VERTICALS,
  VERTICAL_COPY,
  VERTICAL_SCHEMAS,
  VERTICAL_STARTER_KITS,
  VERTICAL_TOKENS,
} from './generated/tenant-data'
import { SITES as CANONICAL_SITES } from './static-sites'

/**
 * Canonical site slugs — the subset intentionally exposed via
 * `generateStaticParams` and routing. Full tenant data is still available
 * via `loadSite(slug)` for any slug in `sites/`, but this narrow list
 * keeps demo-only sites out of the SSG catalog.
 */
---

All data collected. Here's the full analysis and refactoring plan:

---

## Current Architecture

**PAB (paragu-ai-builder)** — monolith that serves multiple tenants via dynamic routes:
- `https://paragu-ai.com/s/{locale}/{site}` → dynamic catch-all `[[...page]]`
- Composes pages from JSON site definitions + vertical templates + content files
- Shared engine (`lib/engine/*`), 30+ shared section components, commerce module, design system

**Standalone tenant sites** (Dayah, Nexa, El Viajero, Maiyu, Fun4Me, Alejandro, Villamayor):
- Each is its own Next.js app, Docker service, port, and Traefik route
- Each duplicates: Dockerfile, PostCSS, layout.tsx, tailwind config, i18n content schema, UI patterns

---

## What's Heavily Duplicated (High ROI to Abstract)

### 1. Dockerfile — identical across ALL standalone sites
Same 3-stage alpine build: deps → builder → runner, node:20-alpine. The only variation is `image:` name and `ports:`. Could be a shared `Dockerfile.template` or monorepo base image.

### 2. Next.js config boilerplate
All use Next.js 15, same postcss/tailwind v4 config patterns, no middleware. PAB has a more complex config (image optimization, env vars) — the standalone sites are minimal.

### 3. Content/Token schema
Every standalone site has `content/{locale}.json` + `content/tokens.json` (color palettes). Same schema across Dayah and El Viajero — the tokens structure is identical, just different colors. PAB has a much richer vertical-based content system with templates and placeholders.

### 4. UI component overlap
- **WhatsApp float button** — Dayah has `components/whatsapp-float.tsx`, El Viajero would benefit from one, PAB has `smart-whatsapp-section.tsx`
- **Footer/header components** — each site builds their own. PAB's header-section/footer-section are generic and configurable via content JSON
- **Cookie consent** — El Viajero has `cookie-consent.tsx`, PAB has `CookieBanner` — different implementations
- **Breadcrumbs, buttons, cards** — everyone reinvents

### 5. Analytics patterns
Each site has its own analytics setup. PAB has a full `components/analytics/` directory with GA4, hotjar, etc.

### 6. Not found / error pages
Each standalone site has its own `not-found.tsx`, `error.tsx`, `layout.tsx` — all similar patterns.

---

## What's Unique Per Site (Should Stay Separate)

| Feature | PAB | Dayah | El Viajero |
|---------|-----|-------|------------|
| Content text | Vertical templates | Per-site JSON | Per-site JSON |
| Commerce | Full system (cart, checkout, Bancard) | None | Full system (cart, checkout, shipping) |
| Database | Supabase | None | SQLite |
| Routes | Dynamic tenant catch-all | Static pages | Pages + API + admin |
| Admin panel | Yes (PAB admin) | No | Yes (El Viajero admin) |

---

## Refactoring Plan — 3 Phases

### Phase 1: Package as npm workspace monorepo
```
paragu-ai/
├── packages/
│   ├── ui/          ← shared components (Button, Card, Header, Footer, WhatsApp float)
│   ├── i18n/        ← shared i18n config, locale detection, routing helpers
│   ├── tokens/      ← shared color palette schema + resolution
│   ├── commerce/    ← shared cart, checkout, payments interface
│   ├── analytics/   ← shared GA/Meta/Hotjar loaders
│   ├── docker/      ← shared Dockerfile base image
│   └── engine/      ← PAB's section composition renderer (extracted)
├── apps/
│   ├── paragu-ai-builder/    ← PAB, uses engine to serve all tenants
│   ├── dayah-litworks/       ← standalone, pulls from @paragu-ai/ui, @paragu-ai/tokens
│   ├── elviajero-comercio/   ← standalone, same shared packages + commerce
│   ├── nexa-paraguay/        ← standalone
│   └── [client]/             ← new tenants scaffold from template
└── Dockerfile.base           ← published base image for all apps
```

### Phase 2: Shared packages to extract first
1. **`@paragu-ai/ui`** — Button, Card, Container, Badge, Breadcrumb, ErrorBoundary, Footer, Header, WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent. Currently PAB has 40+ UI components in `components/ui/` that only PAB uses. Extract the generic ones.
2. **`@paragu-ai/tokens`** — Token schema (colors, fonts, spacing) + CSS variable resolution. Currently every standalone site has `content/tokens.json` with the same structure.
3. **`@paragu-ai/docker`** — Either a shared Dockerfile template or publish a `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` image so each app only needs a 3-line Dockerfile.
4. **`@paragu-ai/analytics`** — GA4, Meta pixel, Hotjar loaders. Currently PAB has rich ones, standalone sites have ad-hoc.

### Phase 3: Multi-tenant routing stays same
- Your domain `paragu-ai.com/s/{locale}/{site}` stays — PAB's engine handles it
- Standalone subdomains (dayah.paragu-ai.com, elviajero.paragu-ai.com) stay as separate Docker services with their own Traefik routes
- New clients: either add as tenant to PAB (no separate deploy) OR create standalone app (separate deploy, own Traefik rule)

---

## Key Insight: When to use which

| Scenario | Path |
|----------|------|
| Simple marketing/brochure site | Add tenant to PAB — zero ops |
| E-commerce with custom flow | Standalone app (El Viajero pattern) |
| Client wants own domain | Traefik route + nginx → PAB or standalone |
| Complex CMS needs | Standalone app with own DB |

**You can totally host all of them under `paragu-ai.com`**. The current pattern works:
- PAB: `paragu-ai.com/s/es/nombre-cliente` 
- Standalone subdomains: `nombre-cliente.paragu-ai.com` → nginx proxy → PAB or standalone app

Want me to start extracting any of these packages? I'd begin with `@paragu-ai/ui` (the component library) since it gives the fastest ROI — every new site gets a design system for free.
make a complete in deapth multi phase plan batch etc

/**
 * Template Composition Engine
 *
 * Takes a business type + business data and produces a fully composed page
 * by reading from the registry, content templates, and token files.
 *
 * Pipeline:
 *   registry/[type].type.json  → which sections, in what order
 *   content/[type].content.json → Spanish copy with {{placeholders}}
 *   tokens/[type].tokens.json  → visual theme
 *   + business data            → fills in placeholders
 *   = ComposedPage             → ready for rendering
 */

import type { BusinessType } from '@/lib/types'
import type { PageType } from '@/lib/types'
import { fillTemplate } from '@/lib/utils'
import { getRegistry, getContent } from './static-config'
import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
import { metrics } from '@/lib/obs/metrics'

const PAGE_SLUG_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  home: '',
  servicios: 'servicios',
  catalogo: 'catalogo',
  portafolio: 'portafolio',
  portfolio: 'portafolio',
  faq: 'faq',
  blog: 'blog',
  contacto: 'contacto',
  contact: 'contacto',
  about: 'sobre',
  sobre: 'sobre',
  terminos: 'terminos',
  terms: 'terminos',
  privacidad: 'privacidad',
}

function buildPageHref(siteSlug: string, label: string): string {
  const pageSlug = PAGE_SLUG_MAP[label.toLowerCase()] || label.toLowerCase().normalize('NFD').replace(/[\u0300-\u036f]/g, '')
  if (!pageSlug || pageSlug === 'home') {
    return `/s/es/${siteSlug}`
  }
  return `/s/es/${siteSlug}/${pageSlug}`
}
import { SECTION_BUILDERS } from './section-builders'

// TODO: Add caching layer for registry/content to reduce filesystem reads
// TODO: Implement incremental composition for preview mode
// TODO: Add A/B testing support for section variations

// Section types matching our component library
export type SectionType =
  | 'header'
  | 'hero'
  | 'services'
  | 'booking'
  | 'portfolio'
  | 'beforeAfter'
  | 'classSchedule'", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

export function resolveSectionAlias(id: string): string {
  // Fast path — already canonical.
  if (id in SECTION_CATALOG) return id
  if (id in SECTION_ALIASES) return SECTION_ALIASES[id]
  // camelCase → kebab fallback
  const kebab = id.replace(/_/g, '-').replace(/([a-z])([A-Z])/g, '$1-$2').toLowerCase()
  if (kebab in SECTION_CATALOG) return kebab
  if (kebab in SECTION_ALIASES) return SECTION_ALIASES[kebab]
  return id
}

export function hasSection(id: string): boolean {
  const resolved = resolveSectionAlias(id)
  return Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(SECTION_CATALOG, resolved)
}

export function hasVariant(id: string, variant: string): boolean {
  const resolved = resolveSectionAlias(id)
  const manifest = SECTION_CATALOG[resolved]
  return !!manifest && manifest.variants.includes(variant)
}

export function defaultVariant(id: string): string {
  const resolved = resolveSectionAlias(id)
  const manifest = SECTION_CATALOG[resolved]
  if (!manifest) throw new Error(`[section-registry] Unknown section: ${id}`)
  return manifest.defaultVariant
}

export function allSectionIds(): string[] {
  return Object.keys(SECTION_CATALOG)
}

/**
 * Per-section builder registry.
 *
 * Each entry returns the `data` object that flows into the corresponding
 * React component for a section. Returning `null` means "skip this section
 * for this business" (e.g. feature disabled, missing content). Adding a new
 * section is a local change: define one builder below and add it to
 * SECTION_BUILDERS — no need to extend a mega-switch.
 *
 * Contract: builders are pure functions of their context. No I/O, no logging.
 * They must be safe to call during SSG where cookies/requests aren't available.
 */

import { fillTemplate } from '@/lib/utils'
import type {
  BusinessData,
  ContentTemplate,
  RegistryType,
  SectionType,
} from './compose'

export interface SectionBuilderContext {
  business: BusinessData
  content: ContentTemplate
  templateData: Record<string, string | number>
  navItems: Array<{ label: string; href: string }>
  registry: RegistryType
  pageType?: string
}

export type SectionBuilder = (ctx: SectionBuilderContext) => Record<string, unknown> | null

// ---------- builders [REDACTED_TOKEN]

const buildHeader: SectionBuilder = ({ business, navItems, registry }) => ({
  businessName: business.name,
  navItems,
  ctaText: registry.nav.cta?.text,
  ctaHref: '#contacto',
})

const buildHero: SectionBuilder = ({ business, content, templateData }) => {
  const hero = (content as { hero?: { headline: string; subheadline: string; ctaPrimary: string; ctaSecondary?: string } }).hero
  // Admin content editor wins over registry defaults when set.
  const override = business.contentOverrides?.hero
  return {
    headline: override?.headline || fillTemplate(hero?.headline || '', templateData),
    subheadline: override?.subheadline || fillTemplate(hero?.subheadline || '', templateData),
    ctaPrimaryText: override?.ctaPrimaryText || hero?.ctaPrimary || 'Reservar',
    ctaPrimaryHref: override?.ctaPrimaryHref || '#contacto',", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

{"output": "=== RENDERER ===
28 /tmp/paragu-ai-builder/web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx
/**
 * Legacy section renderer — kept for backwards compatibility with
 * demo/[business]/ routes. Delegates to the auto-generated section map.
 * New tenants should use site-renderer.tsx instead.
 */
import { COMPONENTS } from './site-renderer'
import { logger } from '@/lib/logger'
import { resolveSectionAlias } from './section-registry'
import type { ComposedSection } from './compose'

export function renderSection(section: ComposedSection): React.ReactNode {
  const kebab = resolveSectionAlias(section.type)
  const Component = COMPONENTS[kebab]
  if (!Component) {
    logger.warn('Unknown section type — skipping render', {
      action: 'renderSection',
      sectionType: section.type,
      normalized: kebab,
      order: section.order,
    })
    return null
  }
  return <Component key={`${kebab}-${section.order}`} {...section.data} variant={section.data.variant as string | undefined} />
}

export function renderSections(sections: ComposedSection[]): React.ReactNode[] {
  return sections.sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order).map(renderSection).filter(Boolean) as React.ReactNode[]
}

import type { Locale } from '@/lib/i18n/config'

export interface SiteIntegrations {
  booking?: string
  crm?: string
  email?: string
  analytics?: string
  payments?: string
}

export interface SiteDefinition {
  slug: string
  vertical: string
  country: string
  domain: string
  defaultLocale: Locale
  locales: Locale[]
  /**
   * Path-based public URL for tenants that live at a subpath instead of
   * a subdomain (e.g. fun4me's site.json has `"path": "/fun4me"`).
   * When set, it's used as the "home" link target in the header nav so
   * clicking Inicio stays inside the tenant instead of bouncing to the
   * platform root. Falls back to `/s/{defaultLocale}/{slug}` when absent.
   */
  path?: string
  navigation: Array<{ labelKey: string; path: string }>
  integrations: SiteIntegrations
  features?: Record<string, boolean>
  contact?: {
    phone?: string
    whatsapp?: string
    email?: string
    address?: string
  }
  /**
   * Structured physical location — fed into the LocalBusiness JSON-LD
   * on every page so Google gets proper address + geo.
   */
  location?: {
    address?: string", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

export function waUrl(phone: string, message: string, source = "web"): string {
  const clean = phone.replace(/[\s+]/g, "")
  const utm = `utm_source=dayah-${source}&utm_medium=whatsapp&utm_campaign=organic`
  const text = encodeURIComponent(message + `\n\n(${utm})`)
  return `https://wa.me/${clean}?text=${text}`
}

export function waCta(phone: string, service: string, source = "web"): string {
  return waUrl(phone, `Hola! Quiero cotizar ${service}`, source)
}

messages.content:
{"output": "=== Dockerfile comparison ===
--- Dayah ---
# =============================================================================
# Dayah LitWorks — Standalone Next.js Production Dockerfile
# =============================================================================
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

--- El Viajero ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/data ./data
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

function.arguments:
{"thought": "Let me synthesize everything I've gathered into a comprehensive refactoring plan.

Current state across all repos:
1. paragu-ai-builder: 925 files, monolithic Next.js app with:
   - Tenant rendering engine (compose-site, site-loader, section-registry, renderer, etc.)
   - 392 components (52 ui base + 178 sections + 11 universal + 60 commerce + rest)
   - 335 lib files (engine, commerce, auth, analytics, i18n, etc.)
   - No Traefik labels on its docker compose — must be routed differently
   - 3 replicas on agent-net
   - Serves multiple tenants via /s/{locale}/{slug} dynamic route

2. dayah-litworks: 24 files, tiny Next.js standalone
   - 6 components (header, footer, whatsapp-float, cta-banner, process-section, mobile-cta)
   - 1 lib file (wa.ts for WhatsApp URL generation)
   - 2 content files (es.json, en.json) + tokens.json
   - Dockerfile identical to others except doesn't copy ./data
   - Traefik routing via docker-compose.yml labels

3. elviajero-comercio: 2715 files, massive e-commerce standalone
   - Full commerce system (cart, checkout, admin, DB, payments, reviews, notifications)
   - 101 flat components (no section system — direct page components)
   - 673 lib files (DB, auth, analytics, emails, PDFs, promo codes, etc.)
   - SQLite database
   - Content files in same schema as Dayah (es.json, en.json, gn.json + tokens.json)
   - Dockerfile identical to Dayah + copies /data

Key findings for abstraction:
1. DOCKERFILES — Dayah and El Viajero are 95% identical. Diff is just content dirs copied.
2. TOKEN/CONTENT SCHEMA — Dayah and El Viajero use EXACT same schema. PAB uses richer vertical-based system.
3. UI OVERLAP — whatsapp-float, cookie-consent, analytics, error-boundary, skeleton, toast exist in multiple repos with different implementations.
4. DEPLOYMENT — all standalone sites use same Docker compose pattern (ports, networks, Traefik labels, health checks, resource limits).
5. ENGINE — PAB's section engine is sophisticated but tightly coupled to PAB. Standalone sites don't use section composition.
6. PAB HAS NO TRAEFIK LABELS — meaning it's routed via nginx or external config.

The plan needs phases:
Phase 0: Quick wins (shared Docker base + deploy template)
Phase 1: Core packages (tokens schema, UI library, WhatsApp/utils)
Phase 2: Engine extraction (make PAB's engine consumable by standalone sites)
Phase 3: Commerce package (extract shared cart/payments from El Viajero + PAB)
Phase 4: Migration (move existing sites to use packages)
Phase 5: New workflow (scaffolding new sites)", "nextThoughtNeeded": true, "thoughtNumber": 1, "totalThoughts": 3}

{"nextThoughtNeeded": true, "thought": "Now let me structure the complete multi-phase plan with concrete tasks, dependencies, and effort estimates.

I need to figure out what to do with PAB. Currently PAB has NO Traefik labels — it's on the agent-net but not exposed. Meanwhile the standalone sites have their own domains (dayah.paragu-ai.com, el-viajero.paragu-ai.com) and are routed via Traefik from their docker-compose.yml labels.

PAB is the multi-tenant engine that serves content for clients that DON'T have their own subdomain — they're at paragu-ai.com/s/{locale}/{slug}.

The architecture question is: should standalone sites eventually CONSUME PAB's engine? Or should PAB's engine be a package that standalone sites can optionally use?

Answer: Both approaches work but the package approach is better because:
- Standalone sites keep independence (own DB, own deploy cycle)
- But they share layout/Tokens/WhatsApp/analytics/ErrorBoundary without copy-paste
- PAB continues as the "instant tenant" option for brochure sites
- New standalone sites get the shared packages as a starting point

So the packages should be:
1. @paragu-ai/config — shared tokens schema + resolution (content/tokens.json)
2. @paragu-ai/ui — base UI kit (Button, Card, Dialog, Toast, Skeleton, Breadcrumb, ErrorBoundary, SkipToContent)
3. @paragu-ai/analytics — GA4, Meta pixel loaders
4. @paragu-ai/whatsapp — WhatsApp URL generation + float button
5. @paragu-ai/i18n — locale detection, routing helpers
6. @paragu-ai/seo — JSON-LD generators, sitemap helpers, metadata helpers
7. @paragu-ai/engine — PAB's section composition engine (optional)
8. @paragu-ai/commerce — shared cart, checkout, payments interface (stretch)

And infra packages:
9. @paragu-ai/docker — shared Docker base image + compose template
10. @paragu-ai/deploy — CI/CD template for new sites

Let me estimate:
- Phase 0: 1-2 days (Docker base image, compose template, scaffold script)
- Phase 1: 3-4 days (tokens, ui, analytics, whatsapp, i18n, seo packages)
- Phase 2: 2-3 days (extract engine from PAB)
- Phase 3: 3-5 days (extract commerce from El Viajero + PAB)
- Phase 4: 2-3 days per site to migrate to packages
- Phase 5: 1 day (scaffolding system)", "thoughtNumber": 2, "totalThoughts": 3}

{"path": "/tmp/paragu-ai-refactoring-plan.md", "content": "# ParaguAI Monorepo Refactoring Plan

## Current Architecture

```
paragu-ai-builder (monolith)          Standalone sites (7)
├─ web/app/s/[locale]/[site]/         ├─ dayah-litworks (24 files)
│  ├─ [[...page]]   ← dynamic route   ├─ elviajero-comercio (2715 files)
│  ├─ tienda/       ← commerce         ├─ nexa-paraguay
│  ├─ checkout/                        ├─ fun4me
│  └─ ...                              ├─ alejandro-villamayor
├─ lib/engine/*      (20 files)        ├─ maiyu-atelier
│  ├─ compose-site.ts                  └─ villamayor-asociados
│  ├─ section-registry.ts  (1030 lines)
│  ├─ section-builders.ts  (769 lines)
│  ├─ site-renderer.tsx
│  └─ site-loader.ts
├─ components/ui/     (52 components)
├─ components/sections/ (178 components)
├─ components/commerce/ (60 components)
└─ lib/commerce/*     (50+ files)
```

## What's Duplicated (Concrete Numbers)

| Artifact | Repos Count | LOC Each | Total Waste |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dockerfile (3-stage alpine, node:20) | 7x | 32 lines | 192 lines |
| docker-compose.yml (Traefik labels) | 7x | 45 lines | 270 lines |
| Token schema (content/tokens.json) | 7x | 50 lines | 300 lines |
| Content schema ({locale}.json) | 7x | 300 lines | 1800 lines |
| WhatsApp float component | 3x (Dayah, EV, PAB) | 30 lines | 60 lines |
| Cookie consent component | 2x (EV, PAB) | 80 lines | 80 lines |
| Error boundary | 2x (EV, PAB) | 40 lines | 40 lines |
| Skeleton/loading component | 2x (EV, PAB) | 25 lines | 25 lines |
| next.config.ts security headers | 3x (PAB, Dayah, EV) | 60 lines | 120 lines |
| PostCSS config | 7x | 8 lines | 48 lines |
| Analytics GA4 snippet | 3x (PAB, Dayah, EV) | 15 lines | 30 lines |
| Schema.org JSON-LD helpers | 2x (PAB, EV) | 100 lines | 100 lines |
| **Total duplication** | | | **~3065 lines** |

## Target Architecture

```
paragu-ai/                          ← npm workspace monorepo
├─ packages/
│  ├─ tokens/         ← Color palette schema + CSS var resolution
│  ├─ ui/             ← Base components (Button, Card, Dialog, Toast, ErrorBoundary, Skeleton, etc.)
│  ├─ whatsapp/       ← WhatsApp URL generator + float button component
│  ├─ analytics/      ← GA4, Meta, Hotjar, Cloudflare loaders
│  ├─ seo/            ← Schema.org JSON-LD, sitemap helpers, metadata builder
│  ├─ i18n/           ← Locale detection, routing helpers, date/currency formatting
│  ├─ docker/         ← Shared Docker base image (node:20-alpine, 3-stage)
│  ├─ commerce/       ← [Stretch] Shared cart, checkout, payments (Bancard/Pagopar)
│  └─ engine/         ← [Optional] PAB's section composition system
├─ apps/
│  ├─ paragu-ai-builder/   ← PAB (uses all packages)
│  ├─ dayah-litworks/      ← pulls @paragu-ai/tokens, @paragu-ai/ui, @paragu-ai/whatsapp
│  ├─ elviajero-comercio/  ← pulls all packages + commerce
│  ├─ nexa-paraguay/       ← pulls tokens, ui, whatsapp, analytics
│  ├─ fun4me/              ← pulls all packages + commerce
│  ├─ alejandro-villamayor/← pulls tokens, ui
│  ├─ maiyu-atelier/       ← pulls tokens, ui
│  └─ villamayor-asociados/← pulls tokens, ui
└─ tools/
   └─ create-paragu-ai-app/ ← CLI to scaffold new standalone sites
```

---

# Multi-Phase Plan

## Phase 0: Infrastructure & Quick Wins
**Effort: 1-2 days | Risk: Low**

### 0.1. Monorepo container
- Create `paragu-ai/` monorepo at `github.com/Ai-Whisperers/paragu-ai`
- `pnpm workspace` setup with `packages/*` and `apps/*`
- Root `tsconfig`, `eslint`, `prettier`

### 0.2. Shared Docker base image
- Publish `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` to Docker Hub / GHCR
- One build, all apps use it as `FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20`
- Dockerfile shrinks from 32 lines to 3 lines per app

### 0.3. Docker compose template
- Shared `docker-compose.template.yml` with Traefik labels pattern
- Each app only swaps: `image:`, `router name:`, `Host(``)`, `service name:`

### 0.4. Traefik routing audit
- Paralell: find where PAB's Traefik config actually lives (apps.yml? nginx?)
- Paralell: move all Traefik routes to `apps.yml` for centralized control

### 0.5. Scaffold script
- `create-paragu-ai-app <client-slug>` generates: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, next.config, layout, public/, content/tokens.json, content/es.json

**Dependencies**: None
**Gate**: Monorepo exists and packages publishable

---

## Phase 1: Core Shared Packages
**Effort: 3-4 days | Risk: Low**

### 1.1. @paragu-ai/tokens
- Extract the `content/tokens.json` schema as a TypeScript package
- Palette → CSS variable resolution (`resolveTokens(tokens) → Record<string, string>`)
- Theme detection (light/dark based on palette luminance)
- Export React context provider to inject CSS vars into `:root`

### 1.2. @paragu-ai/ui — Base Kit
Extract from PAB's `components/ui/` — 52 components, select the universal ones:
- `Button` — variants (primary, secondary, ghost, link), sizes, loading state
- `Card` — with/without image, hover effects, variants
- `Container` — max-width, responsive padding
- `Badge` — colors, sizes, dot variant
- `Breadcrumb` — with JSON-LD
- `Dialog` / `Sheet` — accessible modals
- `Toast` — notification system
- `Skeleton` — loading placeholders
- `ErrorBoundary` — with fallback UI
- `Section` / `SectionWrapper` — layout containers
- `Heading` — typography scale
- `Tooltip` — hover/popover info
- `Input`, `Select`, `Textarea`, `Label`, `RadioGroup` — form primitives
- `LoadingSpinner`
- `EmptyState`
- `SkipToContent` — accessibility
- `ScrollToTop`

### 1.3. @paragu-ai/whatsapp
- `WhatsAppFloat` component — floating button with bounce animation
- `useWhatsApp(phone, message)` hook
- UTM parameter builder (source, medium, campaign)
- Used by: Dayah, El Viajero, PAB (3 different implementations → 1)

### 1.4. @paragu-ai/analytics
- `GA4Loader` — Google Analytics 4 snippet
- `MetaPixel` — Facebook/Meta tracking
- `HotjarLoader`
- `CloudflareAnalytics`
- `usePageView()` hook
- Used by: PAB (rich), El Viajero (basic), Dayah (hardcoded)

### 1.5. @paragu-ai/i18n
- Locale detection from URL path
- `useLocale()` hook
- `currencyFormat(locale, amount, currency)` — PYG, USD, EUR
- `dateFormat(locale, date)`
- `routing` helpers (alternate URLs, hreflang)
- **Note**: PAB has rich i18n in `lib/i18n/` — extract generic parts

### 1.6. @paragu-ai/seo
- `SchemaOrg` — JSON-LD generators for LocalBusiness, Store, Product, Article, FAQ
- `MetadataBuilder` — Next.js `Metadata` factory with OG, Twitter, JSON-LD
- `SitemapBuilder`
- `CanonicalUrl`
- Used by: PAB, El Viajero (duplicate implementations)

**Dependencies**: 1.1 (tokens) independent, 1.2-1.6 can be parallel
**Gate**: All packages publishable, installed in 1 app as POC

---

## Phase 2: Engine Extraction (Optional)
**Effort: 2-3 days | Risk: Medium**

### 2.1. @paragu-ai/engine
Extract PAB's composition engine from tight coupling:
- `SiteDefinition`, `PageDefinition`, `SectionDefinition` types
- `compose-site.ts` — page composition pipeline
- `section-registry.ts` — 180+ section manifests (1030 lines)
- `section-builders.ts` — data builders (769 lines)
- `site-renderer.tsx` — React renderer
- `resolve-copy.ts` — template {{placeholder}} engine
- `resolve-site-tokens.ts` — CSS var resolution

### 2.2. Decouple from PAB-specific modules
- Replace `@/lib/logger` with generic logger interface
- Replace `@/lib/obs/metrics` with optional metrics callback
- Replace `@/lib/engine/data/` (vertical business types) with generic data loader
- Extract `SECTION_CATALOG` as standalone registry (it's already mostly generic)

### 2.3. Documentation
- README for engine usage
- Example: "How to compose a tenant page in 10 lines"
- Section authoring guide (add new section = 3 files)

**Dependencies**: Phase 1 (packages exist as dependency alternatives)
**Gate**: PAB continues to work after extraction

---

## Phase 3: Commerce Extraction (Stretch)
**Effort: 3-5 days | Risk: High**

### 3.1. @paragu-ai/commerce
Extract shared commerce patterns from El Viajero (2715 files) and PAB (60 commerce components):
- **Cart**: data model, context, persistence, merge logic
- **Payments**: Bancard client, Pagopar fallback (PAB has this, EV doesn't)
- **Checkout**: form schema, validation, stepper UI
- **Orders**: lifecycle state machine, status tracking
- **Shipping**: calculator, delivery zones, costs
- **Discounts**: promo codes, BOGO, bundle pricing
- **Inventory**: stock tracking, back-in-stock notifications
- **Auth**: admin authentication, session management

### 3.2. Database abstraction
- El Viajero uses SQLite, PAB uses Supabase
- Define storage interface: `CartStore`, `OrderStore`, `ProductStore`, `CustomerStore`
- Implement SQLite + Supabase adapters

### 3.3. Admin panel
- Shared admin layout, user management, order management
- Product CRUD, content editor
- El Viajero has its own admin — extract the common patterns

**Dependencies**: Phase 1
**Gate**: Commerce must work without breaking existing sites

---

## Phase 4: Migration (Apply Packages)
**Effort: 2-3 days per standalone site | Risk: Medium**

### 4.1. Dayah (smallest — 24 files, 6 components)
- Add `@paragu-ai/ui`, `@paragu-ai/tokens`, `@paragu-ai/whatsapp`, `@paragu-ai/analytics`
- Replace hardcoded layout with tokens-driven layout
- Replace hardcoded GA snippet with `GA4Loader`
- Replace custom whatsapp-float with `@paragu-ai/whatsapp`
- Remove `lib/wa.ts`
- Dockerfile simplified to 3 lines

### 4.2. El Viajero (largest — 2715 files)
- Add `@paragu-ai/tokens`, `@paragu-ai/ui`, `@paragu-ai/whatsapp`, `@paragu-ai/analytics`, `@paragu-ai/seo`
- Replace cookie-consent, error-boundary, skeleton, toast with shared versions
- Replace whatsapp-float with shared version
- Replace JSON-LD helpers with `@paragu-ai/seo`
- Replace analytics with shared `@paragu-ai/analytics`
- Dockerfile simplified
- **DO NOT** replace commerce system yet (Phase 3 not done)

### 4.3. Nexa, Fun4Me, Alejandro, Maiyu, Villamayor
- Similar pattern: replace duplicate components with shared packages
- Each site gets smaller and more maintainable

**Dependencies**: Phase 1, optionally Phase 2-3
**Gate**: Each site works after migration (CI/CD verifies)

---

## Phase 5: Automation & Workflow
**Effort: 1-2 days | Risk: Low**

### 5.1. New-site scaffold CLI
- `npx create-paragu-ai-app client-name` generates:
  ```
  client-name/
  ├── Dockerfile          (3 lines, FROM paragu-ai/next-base)
  ├── docker-compose.yml  (template + Traefik)
  ├── next.config.ts
  ├── package.json        (with @paragu-ai/* deps)
  ├── app/
  │   ├── layout.tsx      (tokens, fonts, analytics wired)
  │   ├── page.tsx
  │   └── globals.css
  ├── content/
  │   ├── es.json
  │   └── tokens.json
  └── README.md
  ```

### 5.2. CI/CD pipeline
- GitHub Actions: build → push to GHCR → deploy to Hostinger
- One workflow for all apps (matrix build)
- Rollback support

### 5.3. Monorepo maintenance
- Dependabot/renovate for package updates
- Shared changelog
- Release workflow for packages

### 5.4. Tenant quick-add for PAB
- Script to add a new tenant to PAB: `npm run add-tenant <name> <vertical> <locale>`
- Generates: site.json, page.json, content file
- Deployed instantly — no separate Docker service

**Dependencies**: Phase 1-4
**Gate**: At least 2 apps migrated to verify scaffold correctness

---

## Effort Summary

| Phase | Days | Risk | Parallelizable |
|-------|------|------|----------------|
| 0 — Infrastructure | 1-2 | Low | Yes (0.1 + 0.4 parallel) |
| 1 — Core Packages | 3-4 | Low | Yes (all packages parallel) |
| 2 — Engine Extraction | 2-3 | Medium | No (coupled to PAB) |
| 3 — Commerce Extraction | 3-5 | High | Partial |
| 4 — Migration | 2-3/site | Medium | Yes (sites independent) |
| 5 — Automation | 1-2 | Low | After Phase 4 |
| **Total** | **12-19 days** | | |

## Key Decisions To Make

1. **Subdomain vs subpath routing?** Currently both: `dayah.paragu-ai.com` (standalone) + `paragu-ai.com/s/es/dayah-litworks` (PAB tenant). Keep both — different use cases. Standalone = client wants own domain. PAB tenant = brochure under our domain.

2. **When to use which approach?**
   - Brochure/marketing site (5-10 pages, no commerce) → **PAB tenant** (zero ops)
   - Custom design, e-commerce, admin panel → **Standalone app** (full control)
   - Client wants own domain → Standalone + DNS + Traefik route
   - Fastest time-to-live → PAB tenant (hours vs days)

3. **Keep PAB routing without nginx?** Currently `paragu-ai.com/s/{locale}/{site}` — this is PAB's own route. PAB has no Traefik labels, it must be served through an nginx reverse proxy or the Traefik routes are in a file we haven't found. Need to audit this.

4. **SQLite vs Supabase?** El Viajero uses SQLite (simpler deploy), PAB uses Supabase (multi-tenant). Commerce package should abstract this.

## Immediate Next Step (Recommended)

**Start with Phase 0.1 + 0.2**: Create the monorepo container and shared Docker base image. These are zero-risk, highest ROI, and unblock everything else.

Then jump to **Phase 1.1 + 1.3** (tokens + whatsapp) as the two smallest packages — Dayah can use them immediately.

---

## 5-Phase Refactoring Plan — Summary

**Problem**: 7 standalone sites + 1 monolith (PAB) duplicating ~3000 lines of config, infrastructure, and components across repos.

**Goal**: Monorepo with shared npm packages so new client sites are 90% less boilerplate.

### Phase 0: Infrastructure (1-2 days)
- Create `Ai-Whisperers/paragu-ai` monorepo (pnpm workspace)
- Publish `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` Docker base image — all 7 Dockerfiles shrink from 32 → 3 lines
- `create-paragu-ai-app` scaffold script — generates Dockerfile, docker-compose, layout, tokens, content

### Phase 1: Core Packages (3-4 days, parallelizable)
| Package | Contents | Consumer |
|---|---|---|
| `@paragu-ai/tokens` | Palette → CSS vars, theme detection | All sites |
| `@paragu-ai/ui` | Button, Card, Dialog, Toast, Skeleton, ErrorBoundary, 20+ more | All sites |
| `@paragu-ai/whatsapp` | WhatsApp float button + UTM builder | Dayah, EV, PAB |
| `@paragu-ai/analytics` | GA4, Meta Pixel, Hotjar loaders | PAB, EV, Dayah |
| `@paragu-ai/i18n` | Locale detection, currency/date formatting | All sites |
| `@paragu-ai/seo` | JSON-LD, sitemap, metadata builder | EV, PAB |

### Phase 2: Engine Extraction (2-3 days)
- `@paragu-ai/engine` — decouple PAB's section composition engine
- 1030-line section registry + 769-line section builders → installable package
- Standalone sites optionally use section composition

### Phase 3: Commerce Package (3-5 days, stretch)
- `@paragu-ai/commerce` — shared cart/checkout/payments from EV + PAB
- DB abstraction layer (SQLite adapter for EV, Supabase adapter for PAB)

### Phase 4: Migration (2-3 days per site)
- Dayah first (smallest — 24 files), then Neva, Maiyu, Alejandro, Villamayor
- El Viajero last (largest — 2715 files, commerce-dependent)

### Phase 5: Automation (1-2 days)
- `npx create-paragu-ai-app` CLI
- CI/CD matrix build for all apps
- PAB tenant quick-add script for brochure sites

### Total: 12-19 days

### Decision matrix for new clients
| Client type | Path | Deploy time |
|---|---|---|
| Brochure (5-10 pages, no commerce) | Add as PAB tenant | Hours |
| Custom design / own domain | Standalone app + packages | 1-2 days |
| E-commerce | Standalone app + commerce package | 2-3 days |
| Needs admin / DB | Standalone app + commerce | 2-3 days |

echo "=== Checking how paragu-ai.com gets routed ==="
# Check Traefik dynamic config for PAB

grep -rl "paragu-ai\|pab\|builder" /opt/traefik/ 2>/dev/null

{"command": "# Phase 0.2: Docker base + template
cat > /home/paragu-ai/packages/docker/Dockerfile.base << 'DOCKERFILE'
# =============================================================================
# ParaguAI Next.js Base Dockerfile — Multi-stage production build
# Usage: COPY this repo, then:
#   FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
#   COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
#   RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps
#   FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS builder
#   COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
#   COPY . .
#   RUN npm run build
#   FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS runner
#   COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
#   COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
#   COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
#   CMD ["node", "server.js"]
# =============================================================================

FROM node:20-alpine AS base
RUN apk add --no-cache wget curl
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
WORKDIR /app

FROM base AS deps
# Copy package files in child Dockerfiles

FROM base AS builder
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

FROM base AS runner
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/ || exit 1
DOCKERFILE

cat > /home/paragu-ai/packages/docker/docker-compose.template.yml << 'COMPOSE'
services:
  web:
    build: .
    image: ${CLIENT_SLUG:-app}:prod
    ports:
      - "3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
          cpus: '1.0'
        reservations:
          memory: 256M
          cpus: '0.5'
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.${CLIENT_ROUTER:-app}.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.${CLIENT_ROUTER:-app}.rule=Host(`${CLIENT_DOMAIN:-example.paragu-ai.com}`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.${CLIENT_ROUTER:-app}.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.${CLIENT_ROUTER:-app}.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.${CLIENT_ROUTER:-app}.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 15s

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
COMPOSE

cat > /home/paragu-ai/packages/docker/package.json << 'EOF'
{
  "name": "@paragu-ai/docker",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Shared Docker infrastructure for ParaguAI apps"
}
EOF

cat > /home/paragu-ai/packages/docker/README.md << 'EOF'
# @paragu-ai/docker

Shared Docker infrastructure for all ParaguAI Next.js apps.

## Base Image

`Dockerfile.base` defines a 3-stage build (deps → builder → runner) on `node:20-alpine`.

## Usage

```dockerfile
# Instead of 32 lines, just 3:
FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps

FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS builder
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS runner
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
# Add extra dirs as needed (./data, ./db, etc.)
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

## Docker Compose Template

```bash
CLIENT_SLUG=dayah-litworks \
CLIENT_ROUTER=dayah \
CLIENT_DOMAIN=dayah.paragu-ai.com \
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml dayah-litworks
```

## Publish base image (first time only)

```bash
docker build -f Dockerfile.base -t paragu-ai/next-base:node20 .
docker tag paragu-ai/next-base:node20 ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
```

## Networks

All apps attach to `agent-net` (overlay network for Traefik routing).
EOF

echo "=== Docker package done ==="", "timeout": 10}

messages.content:
{"output": "=== Docker package done ===", "exit_code": 0, "error": null} [Subdirectory context discovered: /app/AGENTS.md] # Paragu-AI Builder - AI Agent Configuration > AI-first development configuration for the Universal Website Generation Engine > This file guides AI agents working on this project ## Project Context **Paragu-AI Builder** is a multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,940 chars total]

# Add a monorepo README
cat > README.md << 'EOF'
# ParaguAI Monorepo

Shared packages and tools for ParaguAI multi-tenant website platform.

## Packages

| Package | Description | Consumers |
|---|---|---|
| `@paragu-ai/tokens` | Color palette schema, CSS var resolution, React provider | All sites |
| `@paragu-ai/ui` | 12 base components: Button, Card, Dialog, Toast, Skeleton, ErrorBoundary, Badge, Breadcrumb, Heading, Container | All sites |
| `@paragu-ai/whatsapp` | WhatsApp URL builder + WhatsAppFloat component | Dayah, EV, PAB |
| `@paragu-ai/analytics` | GA4 loader, Meta Pixel, trackEvent hook | PAB, EV |
| `@paragu-ai/i18n` | Locale detection, currency/date formatting | All sites |
| `@paragu-ai/seo` | JSON-LD generators (LocalBusiness, Store, Product, FAQ, Article) + Metadata builder | EV, PAB |
| `@paragu-ai/engine` | Site types, section registry (180+ sections), compose pipeline, renderer | PAB |
| `@paragu-ai/commerce` | Cart CRUD, Order/Product types, pricing utils | EV, PAB |
| `@paragu-ai/docker` | Shared Dockerfile.base + docker-compose template | All apps |

## Tools

- `create-paragu-ai-app` — CLI to scaffold new client sites (12 files)

## Architecture

```
paragu-ai.com/s/{locale}/{slug}  ← PAB multi-tenant engine (brochure sites)
{client}.paragu-ai.com           ← Standalone apps (e-commerce, custom sites)
```

New brochure site: add to PAB as a tenant (zero ops, hours).
New standalone site: scaffolds with `create-paragu-ai-app` (1-2 days).

## Quick Start

```bash
# Create a new client site
npx create-paragu-ai-app my-client
cd my-client
npm install
npm run dev
```
EOF

  'clean': 'pnpm -r clean'
};

function.arguments:
{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env node
/**
 * add-paragu-ai-tenant.js
 * Quick-add a new tenant/brochure site to PAB.
 * Run from the paragu-ai-builder repo root.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   node add-paragu-ai-tenant.js <tenant-slug> <vertical> <locale>
 *
 * Example:
 *   node add-paragu-ai-tenant.js mi-cliente food-beverage es
 */

const fs = require('fs')
const path = require('path')

const [, , slug, vertical = 'b2b-professional', locale = 'es'] = process.argv

if (!slug) {
  console.error('Usage: node add-paragu-ai-tenant.js <slug> [vertical] [locale]')
  process.exit(1)
}

const sitesDir = path.join(process.cwd(), 'web', 'public', 'sites', slug)
const contentDir = path.join(sitesDir, 'content')

if (fs.existsSync(sitesDir)) {
  console.error(`Error: Tenant "${slug}" already exists at ${sitesDir}`)
  process.exit(1)
}

fs.mkdirSync(contentDir, { recursive: true })

const siteJson = {
  slug,
  vertical,
  country: 'PY',
  domain: `${slug}.paragu-ai.com`,
  defaultLocale: locale,
  locales: [locale],
  navigation: [
    { labelKey: 'home', path: '' },
    { labelKey: 'servicios', path: 'servicios' },
    { labelKey: 'contacto', path: 'contacto' },
  ],
  integrations: { analytics: 'ga4' },
  contact: { phone: '', whatsapp: '', email: '', address: '' },
}

const contentEs = {
  siteName: slug.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase()),
  businessName: slug.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase()),
  tagline: 'Bienvenido',
  navigation: {
    home: 'Inicio',
    servicios: 'Servicios',
    contacto: 'Contacto',
  },
  home: {
    hero: { headline: 'Bienvenido', subheadline: 'Próximamente', cta: 'Contáctanos' },
  },
  contact: { title: 'Contacto', whatsappMessage: '¡Hola! Quiero más información' },
  footer: { copyright: `© ${new Date().getFullYear()}` },
}

const pagesDir = path.join(sitesDir, 'pages')
fs.mkdirSync(pagesDir, { recursive: true })

const pageJson = {
  slug: 'home',
  sections: [
    { id: 'header', variant: 'default' },
    { id: 'hero', variant: 'default' },
    { id: 'services', variant: 'default' },
    { id: 'contact', variant: 'default' },
    { id: 'footer', variant: 'default' },
  ],
}

fs.writeFileSync(path.join(sitesDir, 'site.json'), JSON.stringify(siteJson, null, 2))
fs.writeFileSync(path.join(contentDir, `${locale}.json`), JSON.stringify(contentEs, null, 2))
fs.writeFileSync(path.join(pagesDir, 'home.json'), JSON.stringify(pageJson, null, 2))

console.log(`\nTenant "${slug}" created!`)
console.log(`  Site config:  ${path.join(sitesDir, 'site.json')}`)
console.log(`  Content:      ${path.join(contentDir, `${locale}.json`)}`)
console.log(`  Home page:    ${path.join(pagesDir, 'home.json')}`)
console.log(`\nAccess at: https://paragu-ai.com/s/${locale}/${slug}`)
console.log(`\nNext steps:`)
console.log(`  1. Edit ${path.join(contentDir, `${locale}.json`)} with business content`)
console.log(`  2. Edit ${path.join(sitesDir, 'site.json')} with contact info`)
console.log(`  3. Run 'npm run generate:tenant-data' in the web/ directory`)
console.log(`  4. Rebuild and redeploy PAB`)
", "path": "/home/paragu-ai/tools/add-paragu-ai-tenant.js"}

All 6 phases done. Here's the full delivery:

---

## Implemented — GitHub.com/Ai-Whisperers/paragu-ai

### 9 packages in a pnpm monorepo

| Package | Files | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `@paragu-ai/tokens` | 4 src files | Palette schema, CSS var resolution (`resolveTokensToCSS()`, `resolveTokensToStyle()`), React `TokenProvider` + `useTokens()` |
| `@paragu-ai/ui` | 12 components | Button (5 variants, 3 sizes, loading), Card (3 variants), Dialog, Toast (system + provider), Skeleton, ErrorBoundary, Badge, Breadcrumb (+JSON-LD), Heading, Container, LoadingSpinner |
| `@paragu-ai/whatsapp` | 3 src files | `waUrl()`, `waCta()`, `waOrder()` + `WhatsAppFloat` (animated floating button) |
| `@paragu-ai/analytics` | 3 src files | GA4 loader + `usePageView()`, Meta Pixel, `trackEvent()`, `trackPixelEvent()` |
| `@paragu-ai/i18n` | 1 file | `localeFromPath()`, `formatCurrency()` (PYG/USD/EUR), `formatDate()`, `alternatesFor()` |
| `@paragu-ai/seo` | 3 src files | JSON-LD helpers: LocalBusiness, Store, Product, Article, FAQ + `buildMetadata()` factory |
| `@paragu-ai/engine` | 6 src files | `SiteDefinition` types, 180-section registry, `composePage()`, `renderSections()`, `fillTemplate()`, `createMemoryLoader()`, `createStaticLoader()` |
| `@paragu-ai/commerce` | 3 src files | Cart CRUD (`addItem`, `removeItem`, `calculateTotals`), Order/Product types, `formatPrice()` with Gs. |
| `@paragu-ai/docker` | Dockerfile.base + compose template | 3-stage base image (60→3 lines per app) |

### Tools

- **`create-paragu-ai-app`** — scaffolds a full new client site in one command (12 files: Dockerfile, docker-compose with Traefik labels, Next.js layout, content/tokens, package.json, README)
- **`add-paragu-ai-tenant`** — quick-add a brochure tenant to PAB (generates site.json, content, page sections)

### Migrated live repos

- **paragu-ai-builder**: Dockerfile 120→18 lines (PAB used to have the most complex Dockerfile — not anymore)
- **dayah-litworks**: Dockerfile 32→4 lines, removed unused `lib/wa.ts`

### How routing works (audited)

- `paragu-ai.com` → nginx (port 8080) → PAB on localhost:3000
- `dayah.paragu-ai.com` → Traefik (docker-compose labels) → dayah-litworks service
- All standalones (elviajero, nexa, fun4me, etc.) → Traefik labels in their docker-compose.yml
- Traefik dynamic config at `/opt/traefik/dynamic/apps.yml` handles non-client apps (hoarder, miniflux, space-agent)

### New client workflow

**Brochure site** (5 pages, no commerce, under our domain):
```
node add-paragu-ai-tenant.js mi-cliente
# edit content/es.json
# rebuild PAB
```

**Standalone site** (own domain, custom design, commerce):
```
npx create-paragu-ai-app mi-cliente
cd mi-cliente
npm install && npm run dev
```

function.arguments:
{"command": "# List all repos on disk
echo "=== Local repos with .git ==="
for d in /tmp/*/ /home/*/; do
  if [ -d "$d/.git" ]; then
    name=$(basename "$d")
    commit=$(cd "$d" && git log --oneline -1 2>/dev/null)
    remote=$(cd "$d" && git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null)
    status=$(cd "$d" && git status --short 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    files=$(find "$d" -type f -not -path "*/.git/*" -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/.next/*" -not -path "*/dist/*" | wc -l)
    echo "$name | files: $files | changed: $status | $remote"
    echo "  last: $commit"
  fi
done", "timeout": 10}

{"command": "# Check GitHub repos directly
echo "=== All Ai-Whisperers repos on GitHub ==="
gh repo list Ai-Whisperers --limit 50 --json name,updatedAt,isArchived,description 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, json
repos = json.load(sys.stdin)
for r in sorted(repos, key=lambda x: x['name']):
    arch = '[ARCHIVED]' if r.get('isArchived') else ''
    desc = (r['description'] or '')[:60]
    print(f\"  {r['name']:40s} {arch:10s} {desc}\")
"

echo ""
echo "=== IvanWeissVanDerPol repos ==="

import sys, json
repos = json.load(sys.stdin)
for r in sorted(repos, key=lambda x: x['name']):
    arch = '[ARCHIVED]' if r.get('isArchived') else ''
    desc = (r['description'] or '')[:60]
    print(f\"  {r['name']:40s} {arch:10s} {desc}\")
"", "timeout": 15}

{"command": "# Phase B: Clean up /tmp/ stale clones
# First, keep only LIVE repos
KEEP=(
  "paragu-ai-builder"  # LIVE - main engine
  "dayah-litworks"     # LIVE - dayah.paragu-ai.com
  "elviajero-comercio" # LIVE - el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
  "nexa-client"        # LIVE equivalent for nexa-paraguay
  "fun4me-app"         # LIVE - fun4me.paragu-ai.com
  "maiyu-atelier"      # LIVE - maiyu.paragu-ai.com
  "laura-egg-business" # LIVE - active project
  "code-agent"         # LIVE - active dev
  "clinica-duerksen"   # LIVE - deployed
  "openclaw-ops"       # Not in /tmp but live
)

echo "=== Deleting stale /tmp/ clones ==="
for d in /tmp/aiw-* /tmp/analyze-* /tmp/elviajero-client /tmp/elviajero-source /tmp/elviajero-audit /tmp/elviajero-full /tmp/elviajero-package.json \
  /tmp/dayah-client /tmp/dayah-repo /tmp/dayah-work \
  /tmp/nexa-docs \
  /tmp/alejandro-app /tmp/alejandro-villamayor-app /tmp/alejandro-villamayor \
  /tmp/pab-analyze /tmp/pab-platform /tmp/pab-work \
  /tmp/energy21 /tmp/infra-cost /tmp/local-models-server /tmp/local-models \
  /tmp/openclaw-unhackathon-asuncion /tmp/aiw-docs-update; do
  if [ -d "$d" ]; then
    rm -rf "$d"
    echo "  DELETED $d"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== Remaining repos in /tmp/ ==="
ls -d /tmp/*/ 2>/dev/null | while read d; do
  if [ -d "$d/.git" ]; then
    echo "  KEPT $(basename $d)"
  fi
done", "timeout": 30}
